Task Name: Add clarification text in Funding Method selection (for cards not displayed)
Task Content:
Context
it was identified during production testing that the users might be adding cards as funding method that are not USA Cards.
The non-USA cards at the momment the funding method needst to be selected, is not displayed, because the Mobile Layer logic "filters" the list of cards and displays only the ones which country matches the corridor card. In this case, the corridors have USA configured as origin country
This situation was confusing to the user because the mobile app will allow to "add" the card, but then the card that was suppousedly added succesfully, is not displayed to be selected as funding method.
It was defined that this situation has two outcomes as solution
1) at a long term, it is necessary to add logic to let the user know that the card is not able to be selected due to the country mismatch, this will be addressed in a further release, see related task: Validate and Inform user if the card country is not correct (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-6563)  
2) at a short term, a clarification text might be added to the funding method selection screen to llet the user know that the cards displayed are only from the origin country allowed, in this case, USA.

UX Details
Text and details to be added by   
 
 Work to be Done
The Mobile layer needs to add the mentioned text to the corresponding screen

Acceptance Criteria
When the user is presented with the selection of the funding method, a text should be present to inform that the funding methods available are the ones which match with the origin country

Task Name: Enhacements for Name handling in Card Registration to improve ANI Match probability
Task Content:
Context
it was identified during testing in production that the handling of the elements of the user name / card holder name when invoking the card registration from CRB were resulting in incorrect name match results.
The aim of the ANI validation shouyld be return at least a partial match, when the ANI was executed, and ignore the result if the ANI was not executed (unknown).
A series of improvements were identified to allow the ANI match for a partial match, as follows:
avoid using the name on card provided by Mobile layer, and "splirtting" this field "By guessing" the first name with the first string.
use the legal name provided by the user in their CIP (throguh a document)
apply the different possible combinations of the legal name in the name elements of the api call to achieve a partial match
avoid handling the "unknown" result as negative outcome, the "unknown" means the ANI was not run by the network, and hence should be simply ignored (this means, with this result the ANI specifically should not result in an invalid card)

Work to Be Done
the platofm should handle the logic to add and validate the card at the payment method creation service, with the following premises
disable the logic around using the "name on card" provided by mobile, in the api call to register the card, altoghether
use the names provided as legal name of the user during their signup (the name obtained by the KYC service): first name, middle name, last name, second last name
If in the first call the ANI results in "Uknown" the validation/ card registration should continue its flow
Execute recursively a combination of these fields for the fields available in the api call to register de card until obtaining a satisfactory result
If the combination provides a "partial match" result, the card should be considered as ani validation passed and the validation/card registration flow should continue its flow.
If the combination provides a no match, the next combination in order should be executed,  
The different combinations to be used as well as their priority, can be reviewed in the following table
 
  
 Acceptance Criteria
when the user is providing a card to be used in the system, either as a funding or receiving card, if by any combination their names are present in the card, and the ANI result return a partial match with any of those combinations, the system should register the card as valid, if all the other validation elements are correct

Task Name: Enhacement for Widget Send to handle CIP through Lola
Lola KYC
Handle new AES Key usage in Incomm Integration
Task Content:
Context
the provider of Cash Load in retailers, Incomm, has informed that they are changing the way the AES encruption is used in their platform

This key is used to encode the primaryAccountNumber field in the generation of the barcode

As a result, the handling of the AES encryption and usage in the platform for barcode generation, needs to be updated

This change needs to be made and rolled out to production prior to February 2026

Work to be Done
The platform needs to udpate the handling of the AES key to use the different encription definition requested by Incomm, see quoted information below:


 InComm is planning to migrate application from AES ECB((Electronic Codebook) mode to AES GCM(Galois/Counter) mode. ECB is considered a weaker encryption mode, and hence, InComm's Compliance Team requested that it migrate to a stronger GCM encryption mode on or before end of February. From InComm's side, the VRS application is ready in all environments, including UAT and PROD.

As a part of the changes, two components will be affected:
1. All request/response parameters currently encrypted should be encrypted using GCM mode.
2. In addition, for the responseCode 97 scenario, currently, the AES key is shared in a plain text format. Going forward, it will be an encrypted value. For this, GCM mode will be used for encryption, and the payload can be decrypted using the previous/recently expired key.

Currently, both ECB and GCM modes are supported. Requesting to plan a change on or before the deadline (end of February). Earlier is better; after the deadline, we will stop supporting ECB mode.

Please find the attached document for the change and code sample. Please let us know if you need any more details.
  
See also attached document = 
 
Acceptance Criteria
this is a general acceptance ticket, for the Cash Load feature. The feature needs to work with no issues and no changes evident to the user

Task Name: Enhace SEON Fraud API call to fine tune the search and avoid receiving unnecessary reponse elements
Task Content:
Context
during the testing performed for the latest changes to include AML analysus in SEON fraud API (see related task) it was identified together with the Development team on one side and the Operations Director on other,  that there were certain enhacements that could be performed to the API call to achieve two objectives:
1) Fine tune the Transaction Analysis, by specifiguying that the AML related data needs to be run in a PERSON (as opposite to UNKNOWN, which is the current value defined in the request). This will ensure that SEON searches/analizes only for person information, instead of also doing it for institution information.
2) Remove unnecesary response elements, by defining in the corresponding field that the only element nedessary to process the response is the id and the state of the transaction analysis, the rest of the information in the response, that can be in occasions a very abundant piece of data, is not processed and hence, not necessary to receive.

Work to Be Done
The platform needs to enhance the curren integration with SEON Fraud API and perform the following changes in the request:
1) Change the following elements/value pair in the request
    "aml": {
            "payment_screening": {
                "sender_config": {
                    "type": "UNKNOWN",
And
                "receiver_config": {
                    "type": "UNKNOWN",
To the following
    "aml": {
            "payment_screening": {
                "sender_config": {
                    "type": "PERSON",
And
                "receiver_config": {
                    "type": "PERSON",

2) Remove all the values in the following element in the request
"response_fields": "state,aml_details,aml_bank_screening_details"
But leave only the following
       "response_fields": "id,state"
Acceptance Criteria
this is a general acceptance feature, overall, the Fraud API Transaction Analysis with SEON should work with no issues

Task Name: Add Fields to FRAUD API in SEON to include execute AML screening
Task Content:
Context
it is necessary to include the feature that SEON has updated, to allow to execute the AML screening for both sender and recipient or a transaction, in the integration for the FRAUD API validation, that is currently executed prior the execution of a transaction.
In order to include the execution of the AML screening, new fields need to be added to the current FRAUD API call, overall:
fixed fields with configuration for the AML screening
new fields for information of the recipient to nurture the AML search

Work to be Done
the Platform needs to add the following fields to the current AML Fraud API
1) a new "config" object
this will allow to send the details of the AML search, this object need to be added with the values "as is"
 "config": {
        "aml_bank_screening": {
            "bank_name_check": true,
            "bank_account_check": true,
            "bank_swift_check": true
        },
    "aml": {
            "payment_screening": {
                "sender_config": {
                    "type": "UNKNOWN",
                    "sources": {
                        "sanction_enabled": true,
                        "pep_enabled": true,
                        "watchlist_enabled": true,
                        "crimelist_enabled": true,
                        "adversemedia_enabled": true
                    },
                    "fuzzy_enabled": true
                },
                "receiver_config": {
                    "type": "UNKNOWN",
                    "sources": {
                        "sanction_enabled": true,
                        "pep_enabled": true,
                        "watchlist_enabled": true,
                        "crimelist_enabled": true,
                        "adversemedia_enabled": true
                    },
                    "fuzzy_enabled": true
                }
            }
        },
        "aml_api": true,
        "aml_bank_screening_api": true,
        "response_fields": "aml_details,aml_bank_screening_details"
    },
2) refined information of the receiver
these fields need to be added to the current fields in the receiver section, that was only sending the receiver full name
    "receiver_firstname": "First Name of the Receiver",
    "receiver_middlename": "Last Name and if available second last name of the receiver",
    "receiver_lastname": "Last Name and if available second last name of the receiver",
    "receiver_dob": "date of birh ot the receiver",
    "receiver_country": "country of the receiver - two letter iso code",   


Note from TA / Acceptance Criteria 
the handling of the transaction results (the response of the API) and the events triggered will continue working as is today, because when either the recipient or the sender have an AML hit, the SEON platform will set the transaction in Review.
When the transaction is in review in SEON, the Platform sets the transaction on hold waiting for Risk Analisis, and it will be responsability of the Risk Team to either decline or approve the transaction.
The change of status at SEON triggers the events as they do currently,
The handling of the events does not change

For testing purposes, SEON must be enabled in QA environemtn and the transaction needs to be have an amount 25 and maximun 475. You can use known hits in AML list such as Joaquin Guzman and associates

Task Name: Prevent the user to send the Billing Addres info multiple times
Task Content:
Context
When the user is in the flow to add a card, and the first validation returns AVS fail, the user is required to provide a billing address instead (see screenshot).
If the user tapped -by mistake, maybe- multiple times in the "Confirm address" button, the request to add a card was sent multiple times, causing the backed layer to add the card, one time,  but eventually sending an invalid card event to the mobile layer due to the concurrency of the requests.
This caused the user to be informed that the card was invalid, on one side, and then visualizing the added card within their valid payment methods, which was extremely confusing

Work to be Done
to avoid this isusue, the mobile layer needs to modify the interaction of the confirm address button to allow the user to tap it only once

Acceptance Criteria
the user will be able to add a billing address, and confirm such address only once, in csae of AVS fail, and the card should be validated with no issues
If the card is valid the card will be added and be usable in transactions, if not, the user should be informed properly and the card not available to be used.

Task Name: Add new fields to Arcus's payments API to comply with SPEI Remmitances (Transaction 36)
Task Content:
Context
as per a new guideline enforced by Mastercard, the remmitances to mexico that have as destination a SPEI, need to comply to have the sufficient information for this type of transaction (called transaction 36)

See attached documentation for Full Context

See below Product Requirement

Product Requirement Description: 
Add SPEI Remittance Fields to Create SPEI Remittances Payment in Arcus API
Arcus is enabling Transaction Type 36 as a payment type to classify SPEI transactions as remittances correctly. This ensures compliance with Banxico's AML and Compliance procedures. Beginning October 27, 2025, the use of Transaction Type 36 will be mandatory for all remittance-related payments.

To comply, Leap must update the Arcus API integration (Create SPEI Remittances Payment) to include the following required fields in the payload for all remittance transactions:

remittance_type
remittance_id
country
currency
foreign_provider
national_provider
ex_rate

Important: Failure to comply may result in rejection of remittance transactions by certain SPEI participants.

Deadline:
October 27, 2025

Reference:
Arcus Email Communication – October 8, 2025
Documentation link: https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/payments---spei-remittances/wz3hipv4tfwam-create-spei-remittances-payment

Work to be Done
The platform needs to include the fields mentioned in the product requirement to the curent payments API call, with the following detail of the field values

"remittance_type": "inbound", -- fixed value
    "remittance_id": "RMMX", -- the id of the transaction in Numi Platform
    "country": "US", -- origin country, fixed value as it's always US
    "currency": "USD", -- origin currency, fixed value as it's always USD
    "foreign_provider": "Leap Financial", -- fixed value, leap financial is the foreign provider
    "national_provider": "IFPE KLU TECH", -- fixed value, this is the corresponsal bank
    "ex_rate": "19.2500" -- exchange rate of the transaction


The following needs to be considered 
1) The flow of the Arcus SPEI Transaction  will be executed as it currently does invoking the    API
2) The Response will be handled as currently with no changes, 
3) The response will contain an "echo" off the mentioned fields, as described in the reference documenation - this last piece can be taken as validation piece for testing purposes

Considerations for QA Team
This taskj should be handled as general acceptance of the "send to UBN" scenario
since the fields cannot be tested, we need to ensure that the changes made do not jeopardy the current functionality

Task Name: Add Fix for VGS Vault configuration to handle 503 Errors
Task Content:
Context
due to certain errors experienced by QA team in the adding of the card it was identfied that the tokenization process was not properly configured at VGS Vault
The upstream url was a generic development tool url
This caused that VGS will not be able to execute the tokenization due to the failed of the upstream url (it returned 503)
After investigation it was defined that the approach to fix this should be implementing a service that is published as http in one of our servers, and use this service as the actual url of the upstream route.

Work to be Done
The Platform need to implement a Service in all the environments
The VGS Vault configurations for all the environments and projects (HWAY and RAAS) need to be updated with the corresponding configuration
Linked Tasks: Enhacement to obtain Card Network from Add Card CRB Response: https://app.clickup.com/t/86dxqcbcd)

The VGS Vault need to be configured to acces the upstream url of the new service
The users in all the environments shjould be able to add cards using the VGS-collect form or the tokenization POST with no issues
The tokenization process should work with no issues

Task Name: Update Limits on Card Add Attempts Per User
Task Content:
Context
it was identified in production testing that the limits established of adding card per user was too low, given that some cards might be blocked because they are not valid, and it is needed to provide more attempts to the user

Work to Be Done
The platform needs to update the limit that affects the number of Cards to be added in a certain amount of time, needs to be changed, with the definitions of the product requirement detailed below

Note from TA:
when the user reaches the limit the UX is defined in another task = Enhacement to UX when user reaches amount of cards allowed in a period (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-5014)

Product Description:
We currently enforce limits on the number of card add attempts per user. These thresholds need to be updated to better align with our fraud prevention strategy and reduce false positives.

Updated Requirements:
7-Day Limit:
Modify the existing rule to allow a maximum of 5 card add attempts per user within any rolling 7-day window.

30-Day Limit:
Modify the existing rule to allow a maximum of 7 card add attempts per user within any rolling 30-day window.

Behavior (No change) - Scope & Notes:

The limit applies to all card add attempts, successful or failed.
Once the user reaches the threshold, additional attempts must be blocked.
Continue to display the current message or update.
This is an update to current functionality — not a new feature.
Applies globally across all environments and partners using Leap.
Ensure existing logs and alerts reflect the updated thresholds.

Task Name: Ignore AVS Responses from CRB Validation on Receiver Cards
Task Content:
Context
it was identified that the platform had the logic to check the AVS response from CRB when the card was going to be used for Push (OCT) transactions.
This was an error introduced by the fact that originally both card validation / add flows were in fact only one, and AVS is necessary for the cards that are going to use for Pull (AFT)
Afterwards, when the validation logic was separated depending on the use of the card, the AVS part was only meant to be kept in the AFT side, but was mistakinlgy kept also in OCT

Work to be Done
The Platform need to fix the logic to process AVS only in AFT card validation, and not in OCT

Product Description:
Currently, AVS (Address Verification System) responses from CRB are being processed for both sender and receiver card validations.

Requirement:
Update the logic to ignore AVS responses from CRB for receiver cards. AVS validation should only apply to sender cards, as the address verification is irrelevant for disbursement methods involving receiver cards.

Reason:
Receiver cards are not subject to address-based validation logic in Leap’s disbursement process.

This change prevents unnecessary validation errors or rejections for transactions where AVS is not meaningful.
Latest Comment: Closing this ticket as it was tested succesfully in PROD by Hector
Lists: [CRB Requirements, Technical Backlog, Technical Requirements - TA RaaS, 1.12.5 PEPPER (Address Enhcm)]
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text):
When the user is adding a card to be used as a destination payment method (push funds - OCT) the AVS result returned by CRB will be ignored, this means either if its true of false, will not have impact in the process of validating the card
This feature cannot be tested in Sandbox from CRB

Task Name: Process the information when the user provides NON standardized Address format
Task Content:
Context
it was idenfied that certain countries that are in the process of being added to the system for recipient users (either when the users are defining their own data in the ASK app or when the sender is defining recipient data in the SEND app), do not have a "structured" format for the address (they do not respondo to the ussual = street name, number, locality, city, format). This is mostly a culture issue because the people is not used to provide a structured address, but also may present a challenge when defining it through GLS

Work to be Done
The backend layer needs to be able to identify the most elements from an Address that was not standarized, with the data provided by the Mobile layer = latitude and longitude (see related tasks)
The platform will take the information sent by the Mobile layer of the user's selected map pin, latitude and longitude, and try and identify a set of more precise data to store, including fields such as:
street name
street number
city

For the time being this new Address Capture needs to be presented when the Address is in the following countries (including the Tennant that is affected by this):
Bolivia - Financredits
Paraguay - Financredits

Guatemala
Honduras
El Salvador
Nicaragua

This "different" UX for the Address Capture will be displayed upon a configuration at country level. If this configuration is not setted for the county, the Address Capture will be the regular existing one.

See UX Details for Reference
 
What do we have to do??
In this cases instead of starting looking for the address in the google search, you go directly to the map. In the address confirmation in the field "Apt, Suite, Floor" change it to Address description, where the user can write a more precise description of the address 
 
*Note: this will only be available for countries that do not have a standardized address format.

  

 



See related document for more Context  Handle non standarized Address (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41477)
Latest Comment: ajustado
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text):
When the sender user is defining the data of the recipient in the SEND app, or if the recipient user is defining their own data, at the moment of entering the address, if the Address is in the defined countries, the Address Capture experience will be "non -structured" allowing the user providing details in a "free text" form, and capturing the address directly from the map. The countries that are not defined in this task, will continue to have the regular Address Capture with the ussual Address form.
The backend needs to be able to translate the information provided by Mobile layer (lat and long) of the user's Map pin selection, into a most accurate Address information to be stored in the platform. The information of the Address will then be as complete as possible in the user's records in the platform (e.g. the different address fields should be as complete as possible in the user's profile to be reviewed in the console)

Task Name: hot fix - Handle Events Collision in Transactions with Card
Task Content:
Context
during the testing of the integration with landing port, there were errors reported in the completion of the transaction data and the execution of these transactions.
The errors were caused by a collision of Platform Events in the following situation:
the UBN Card payment method is being added to the transaction
the Card payment method is being set in the Landing Port
This was later identified as also happening in ASK apps in the following scenario
The recipient user is registering their payment method of type Card right after finishing its onboarding, in the ASK app.

Work Done
A hot fix was added to QA to handle the events collision and avoid the Transaction to be processed wrongly.
This change fixes both the issue found in the landing port and the ASK app

Task Name: Enhacement to handle non-english Google Places Attributes in Platform Mappings for Address elements
Task Content:
Context
see Handle multi language State short name (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41417) 

Work to be Done
When receiving the information of the Address, the Numi Platform will use the latitud and longitude received, and then invoke the "reverse location API" with the mentioned data.
➡️The reverse location API can be invoked with a parameter that will define the language the data should be retrieved. The parameter will be set in the official language of the country (e.g, for Brazil, the official language is Portuguese)
From the data retrieved by the reverse location API, the "short name" of the State, will be extracted.
This information will be set in the Numi Platform Address object, more specifically in the Address 3 field, replacing its original value (returned in the original address capture).
The mappings of the state to be used with the different PRoviders, should now  work properly.
This procedure needs to be applied in all the instances where the Address is captured and creates an Address Object related to a user in the Platform:
User CIP Level 1 Execution/Completion
Landing Port Integration to User CIP Level 1 Completion
Send and Get creation of Automatic User
Billing Address Capture for the Information/Validation of Cards

Task Name: Execute Call to SEON Transaction Analysis
Task Content:
Context
Leap Risk team is using a new tool / provider called SEON see= SEON (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41217)
As part of the integration with SEON, This Tool will analyze the data from the Numi Platform Transaction performed by an API Call before the Transaction is Executed
The following transactions
any transaction with Card Load as a funding / source payment method (also called Pull from Card or AFT)
any transaction with any other funding method except Cash
Other? (tbc from   /   
Needs to be sent to SEON for Analysis trhough an API Call to their FRAUD Engine
Depending on the outcome of the Fraud API, the continuity of the transaction life cycle might be affected.

 Work to Be Done
The platform needs to execute the Call to SEON Fraud Engine with the details of the transaction in the fields defined in the related document
The call to the API needs to be executed before the transaction is funded in the platform, and only when the transaction has Card as Source Payment Method

it ws defined with Risk team that if any data of the required for this API call is not available at the moment of execution (e.g. because the receipient does not exist yet or the recipient payment method is not defined) the field will not be sent


Depending ont the response of the API call, the following needs to happen
if the API returns APPROVED - the transaction can continue with their lifecicle
if the API returns REVIEW - the transaction needs to be set on hold and the mechanism to wait for the Weebhook needs to be triggered
There will be a new status detail for the Hold status, should be called something like "OnHoldRiskAnalysis" or similar
if the API returns REJECTED - the transaction will be set to cancelled, with the corresponding specific statusDetail

Note: 
the outcome of SEON analysis when the Analysis returns a REVIEW outcome, will be triggered in Events that need to be handled, this mechanism is defined in the following task = [FRAUD TOOL] - Process Events from SEON (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4203)
Linked Tasks: [FRAUD TOOL] - Message to User when Fraud API Declines Transaction: https://app.clickup.com/t/86dvnha7u)
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text):
When a user (either send or request) executes a transaction, the transaction information should be send to SEON Fraud API and depending on the response the transaction might be cancelled, might continue of might be set on hold. 
For Testing purposes in order to reproduce different scenarios,  review the Testing Scenarios documentation

Task Name: Remove mechanism of transaction cancellation if error occurs on exchange rate provider, when destination payment method is OCT
Task Content:
Context
it was identified that the mechanism of transaction cancellation when the exchange rate provider throws error, does not apply when the destination payment method does not involve an actual money movement with a provider to buy local currency, and hence the exchange rate used in the transaction is merely informative.
For example for destination payment method card, the OCT operation, does not need the exchange rate becuase it executed in USD, and the exchange rate is informative, because the recipient's issuing bank is ultimately the one that actually applies the exchange to the local currency
In the other hand, for the destination payment method UBN, there's a MSB involved that provides the local currency exchange, so the exchange rate used in the transaction needs to be informed to finance -through a report- to later perform the money movement to settle the transactions with the MSB outside the platform.

Work to be Done
The platform needs to add the necesary enhacements to avoid the cancellation of the transaction because it was included in the mechanism of re-attempts  if the provider of exchange rate throws error, when the destinatin payment method is card, and hence the OCT transaction is executed.
FOR OCT Transactions the exchange rate fetch errors will not affect the lifecycle of the transaction.

Task Name: Implement OFAC Screening Endpoint in CRB API
Task Content:
Context
it is necesary to add the integration of a new endpiont from the CRB PTPE APIs to be able to provide the OFAC Screening rquired from CRB to the OCT Cross border tranactions

Work to be Done
The Platform needs to implement the call and handing of the results of the Ofac Screening API from CRB
This new service will be then used by the OCT flows to identify if the OCT Transaction can be performed or not
In order to to this the followint needs to happen: 
1) The platform needs to provide the following in the API request=
requestID = the  unique identifier of the request, a guid, as already in use for other endpoints for CRB
sourceSenderId = the identifier sent as part of the credentials of the part of the configuration of the CRB API, 
cardToken = the token obtained from the call to the Card endpoint executed when the recipient card is added, and validated
2) The platform needs to handle the following in the API response=
status field
returns : "Hit"  = this means the the service needs to inform that the transaction cannot continue

returns: "Allowed"  = this means the service needs to informa that the transaction can continue

Other Scenario: In the improbable cause that the card is identified by the Ofac SCan as a domestic card, the OfacScan API may throw the following error = "Credit card UMHBXBBMCFNPYO0DBNI9A82A7CFCL5QE17ZZIA is not international. No need validation in ofac list."
In this particular case, the platform needs to handle this response as an "allowed" card, meaning, needs to inform that the transaction can continue

URL in sandbox of the API
 
Example CURL
curl --location "https://pushtopaystaging.crbnj.net/api/OfacScan" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--header "Accept: application/json" ^
--header "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6IjdhYVdOTnU0cGdVVHNUemkxLUZYdGZ2NUVtNCIsIng1dCI6IjdhYVdOTnU0cGdVVHNUemkxLUZYdGZ2NUVtNCIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..MUG5_VWMouFtxcXTCZ_tQyIjMiXF8xuKD63zNh08dgI3rsNZDC2dOXhmX2XP8N0ewc7LLtg-Y6IjTi1Gy61MmGfWgoZW1Vz142tawy0RjClFr9r3alV6DG_tU53Dx_UFdafcZHF4L10xDgVmsPSpNgOewYRUUPDSGI-wF7eSpYbNfBqqT18MQvNvRnCPwLRx6aqfDAfq5a8XHfpUVJDNRWNtXofhRiaeTVzaPBD0GRlKBsRshM75yKqtgJgMwq32iWHY9n7FQUv_eGOURsjqr2opOijW4O8iaFngiC8-tW0VNfxZX_H2607PEXm_GN18mH9MY3yRXxlSORMw8IM-_A" ^
--data "{ 
     \"requestId\": \"7c8fc9c1-65a9-4878-aef7-b6adbdacd265\",
    \"cardToken\": \"UMHBXBBMCFNPYO0DBNI9A82A7CFCL5QE17ZZIA\",
}"
Example Response card not International*
{
    "version": "1.0.0.0",
    "result": {
        "errors": [
            {
                "code": 1000,
                "message": "Credit card UMHBXBBMCFNPYO0DBNI9A82A7CFCL5QE17ZZIA is not international. No need validation in ofac list."
            }
        ]
    },
    "isSuccessfull": false,
    "isSuccessful": false
}
*Note= this error happens on sandbox, on all cards, see related doc to obtain more infromation


Example Response of Allowed Card (working)
{
  "version": "1.0.0.0",
  "result": {
    "requestId": "7c8fc9c1-65a9-4878-aef7-b6adbdacd265",
    "creditCardToken": "1E83D5A53BA24D2A847A52AAD63A7C44000000",
    "error": null,
    "requestedAt": "2025-04-23T17:56:44.0434807Z",
    "respondedAt": "2025-04-23T17:56:44.8400317Z",
    "updatedAt": "2025-04-23T17:56:44.8400317Z",
    "status": "Allowed"
  },
  "isSuccessfull": true,
  "isSuccessful": true
}

Example Response of Card with Ofac Hit
{
  "version": "1.0.0.0",
  "result": {
    "requestId": "7c8fc9c1-65a9-4878-aef7-b6adbdacd267",
    "creditCardToken": "44A10BB323C748A0B0800C71695BC05A000000",
    "error": null,
    "requestedAt": "2025-04-23T20:29:50.1886016Z",
    "respondedAt": "2025-04-23T20:29:50.9330995Z",
    "updatedAt": "2025-04-23T20:29:50.9330995Z",
    "status": "Hit"
  },
  "isSuccessfull": true,
  "isSuccessful": true
}

Response Fields information (from documentation)
requestId
String
Matches the RequestId found in the request for information on international transaction screening

creditCardToken
String
A string of randomly generated characters for the payment card the consumer signed up for. Length = 38 characters (alphanumeric).

error
String
A numeric code that describes any error that could happen during the OFAC compliance check

requestedAt
String
The date and time you requested the OFAC transaction.  In this case, the date and time are in this format: yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss[.mmm]

respondedAt
String
The actual date and time the OFAC service sent the response.  In this case, the date and time are in this format: yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss[.mmm]

updatedAt
String
The date and time Cross River received the OFAC status change after checking for cardholders that could be matches (“hits”). Verify whether transaction was allowed or rejected. Status changes not yet available.  In this case, the date and time are in this format: yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss[.mmm]

status
String
"Hit" - either the entity is a suspect on the OFAC list, or the check is still in progress.  "Allowed" - the entity can transact.


Full Documentation of the API

Task Name: Include CRB OFAC Screening for OCT Cross Border
Task Content:
Context
In order to perform the Cross Border OCT Transactin, the CRB bank that provides the OCT transactions service, has requested to include the ofac screening for the recipient card, before executing the transaction.

Work to Be Done
The platform needs to include a call to the service to recipient's card ofac screening right before the execution of the actual OCT Transaction.  See related task for the technical implementation of the New OFAC Screen endpoint in Platform's CRB API = Implement OFAC Screening Endpoint in CRB API (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4930) 
If as a result of the ofac screening the card returns a hit in black lists, the transaction needs to be set on hold, the OCT will not be executed,  and a ticket needs to be sent to Operations Desk. See related story for Operations Ticket Trigger = Trigger Operations Ticket when OFAC Screening returns hit in recipient's Card (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4931) 
If the ofac screening does not return hit in the recipient's card, the transaction can be finished and the OCT is executed

Follow up validations and process
When the Operations Teams executes a manual process to validate with CRB compliance team,  there could be two outcomes of the transaction =
if the recipíent's card resulted in a false hit, the operation shouyld be able to be continued by the retries mechanism present in the Operations Console, and the transaction needs to be continued normally
if the recipient's card resulted in a positive hit, the operations team should be able to cancel the transaction using the mechanism present in the Operations Console, and the transaction should be terminated.
Lists: [Technical Requirements - TA RaaS, Kanban R&D, 1.11.7.6 Magnolia Bug Fixing]
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text):
When there's a crossborder OCT transaction, this means, the transaction will executed to push funds to a non-usa card, before the actual execution of the OCT Transaction, the OFAC Screening for CRB needs to be executed.
If this screening results in a hit, then the transaction will be set on hold, if the screening does not result in a hit, then the transaction will continue normally
Also, if the transaction is set on hold, a ticket should be triggered to the Operations team. Review the related tasks mentioned for the rest of the scope of this feature.

Task Name: Invex Anti-Terrorism Requirements
Task Content:
Context
due to the changes in the legal requirements in USA in Trump administration for Cross Border money transactions with Mexico, the partner Invex has required certain changes to be implemented in their integration to comply with this legal requierements and avoid sanctions.
The changes are related to the way the information of the Mexican user is sent to Invex SFTP, from the Platform's RAAS Middleware layer:
1) the moment the file is sent is now at the end in the lifecicle of the user's CIP Process, to make sure all the information is sent before Invex creates the account for the user.
2) the checksum that the file is encripted with, needs to be sent separatedly

Work to be done
The RAAS Middleware layers needs to update the mechanism that capture and prepare the data of the "expediente" in order to comply with the requirements, 
sent the prospect (a.k.a "expediente") files, before making the call to Invex to create the account. This will be achieved by uploading the photo id and selfie files to the SFTP as soon as the user captures their photo ID, this is, when the user completes their CIP Level 2 (instead of the current moment, that is, after the user reaches CIP level 1)
add a new field to the service that notifies invex that "prospect file" is ready, with the information of the checksum that the file is encripted with

Task Name: Error in FX usage and calculations for Quote and Tx Execution
Task Content:
During the Production Testing for CRB it was identified that there were errors in the calculations in the quote with the usage of the exchange rate

Issue Found
The details of the findings are as follows:

Findings y Conclusiones prueba - Issue de Montos
inconsistencia entre los valores que se hacen request / send (lo que se ve en e flujo) / el monto que se hace efectivo finalmente
los fixes que se hicieron en el quotation (Natalia) parecen estar fallando 
se necesita un analisis con backend para ver que pasó lo del quotation 
es una suposición
revisar que se hace diferente allí de al momento de aplicar la transaction
revisar el momento en el que se toma la FX de la DB, si en el Quote se hace ésto está mal, en el Quote y en la ejecución del a TX, se debe tomar el FX real provisto online por el provider (no el guardado) 
Aclaraciones de Claudia respecto del FX
Pre-Quote: lo trae de la DB
Quote: lo trae del Proveedor (Euroexchange-Hardcoded) -- tiene los basis points
Corridor / si no funciona el provider, trae la DB
Transaction: Proveedor (Euroexchange-Hardcode) deberia tener tambien los basis points (Claudia menciona que parece que no se está haciendo eso) 
Conclusiones Hector
--- no se esta usando el mismo FX ni calculo en el quote y en el momento de ejecutar la transaction
--- cuando es un send - se debe respetar lo que el enviador dijo que tenia que mandar : no somos responsables del tipo de cambio que le llega al receptor
---- cuando es un request - se debe respetar el importe solicitado, en tanto no afecte lo que lo que se cobró al sender

Expected:
The amount informed in the Quote and the amount used in the Execution of the transaction should be calculated with the FX provide online by the provider, not the one stored in the DB
The amount informed in the Quote and  the amount used in the Execution of the transaction should include the "Basis Points" in the calculation
When there is an error that does not allow to obtain the online FX, the implemented on the following ticket must occur Cancel operations when Exchange Rate cannot be retrieved for several hours (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4073)

Task Name: Handle Email parameter for CRB Onboarding
Task Content:
Context
When the user finisheds their CIP process and the platform continues to the CRB onboarding process ,the email parameter is a non required information in the CRB API
Currently the platform was not handling this and was sending a random email instead
When the user has not provided an email in its onboarding the information should not be sent to CRB system for Onboarding

Work to be done
The platform needs to analyze if the email information is present as part of the user's CIP information and if its not present the element in the API that fills the information of the email in CRB system, should not be sent
The element is the following

    "primaryEmail": {
        "isPrimary": true,
        "emailType": "Personal",
        "emailAddress": "vanesa@leapfinancial.com"
    },

The random email feature that was being used, should be removed

Task Name: Card Validation Process for OCT with CRB
Task Content:
Context
During the first round of testing for the Card Validation Process it was discovered that the mentioned process needs to be applied "as is" only for cards that are intented to use for thethe AFT (load with card) flow

The Card Validation for the cards to be used in OCT (send to card) flow need to undergo a slighty different process, so it should be considered as a different feature

See full feature documentation = Card Validation Process for OCT (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39617) 

Work to Be Done
The Overal Card Validation Process for OCT needs to occur as following in the platform (note, the strikethrough items will not be implemented for CRB due to technical inabilities from the CRB APIs)
•	Check Platform Bin records
•	Execute Bin Lookup (Get Card Capabilities)
see Execute Bin Lookup with Cybersource (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4202) 
•	Perform micro deposit if applicable
•	Perform Account Ownership Verification 
o	Get ANI results
o	In-house Name Matching if applicable
•	Update Platform Bin Records if applicable
Mark the card as validated for OCT

 If in any step the information is conclusive that the car is NOT valid, the card will not be accepted and not be able to be used and the user should be informed properly

Task Name: UX when Sender defined Receiver's Card and said card needs Micro-Deposit Validation
Task Content:
Context
There was a missing scenario not considered, when the Sender has defined the destination payment method, and such payment method is a card, and such card undergoes the Card Validation process (see Card Validation Process for AFT (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237) )

At a certain point of the card validation process, and depending the outcome of the different steps IT MIGHT be needed for the card holder to complete the micro deposit information

Since in this scenario the user (the card holder) did not define the card himself, but it was defined by the sender user, the User Experience needs to be specialized to handle this situtation

Work to Be Done
The UI needs to handle the status of the card for "micro-deposit needed" (see with @Alvaro the correct status name)
The UX should be designed to let Recipient user know, that he needs to complete the micro deposit information and continue to finish the card Validation and receive their money. Also, the UX lets the sender know that the receiver needs to validate their card.

Task Name: Fix Load with Card + Send Card Flow to consider the new card validation scheme
Task Content:
Context
When the flow that loads with card and then sends to card was reviewed it was found that the New Card Validation Process was not considered
This taks aims to cover the missing steps to use the New Card Validatin Process (see Card Validation Process for AFT (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237) ) as part of the flow when the sender user also decides to define the recipient payment method (card)  to validate the destination card.

Work to be Done
The platform needs to call the add card service with the corresponding connection to the new card validation process
The platform needs to modify the mentioned flow (Load with Card + Send to Card) to include the handling of the different outcomes of the card validation:
Continue with the flow if the card was validated succesfully
Pause the flow and wait for the micro deposit when the validation needs this specific step (see UX when Sender defined Receiver's Card and said card needs Micro-Deposit Validation (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4186) )
Fail the card if the card validation process outcome is not succesfull, and set the flow to wait for a new payment method


Note from TA, the related ticket for the specific scenario of micro-transaction needed on the recipient's card, is OOS of this task

Task Name: Replace previous rollback mechanism for a push transaction when an operation is cancelled
Task Content:
Context
Due to the fact that the CRB integration for AFT and OCT does not allow to rollback the transaction in either flow, it is necessary to find a way to return the funds to the credit card when the transaction needs to be cancelled
The best way to return the funds is take advantage of the push to card transaction available within the same CRB features, even when a cost is involved, it addresses the issues caused by not being able to return the funds inmediatly (operational and legal issues)

Work to be Done

The push transaction will not include the fees of the original AFT Transaction
The scenarios where this push transaction needs to be executed are as follows
when the sender user is cancelling their operation manually (and if the operation is in a status that allows the cancellation)
when the cancellation job is executed

Task Name: Perform OCT Push Micro Transaction as part of New Card Validation Process
Task Content:
The integration with CRB systems to register and validate cards turned out to be insufficient from a risk / safeguard stand point, to avoid accepting cards that might be stolen.

The CRB also has a certainly poor handling of the possibilitries of the transactions with cards, not allowing the reversals

Because of this situation, it is necessary to add certain features that were developed "in house" to leverage a more accurate desition making in the card validation process.

See the full Card Validation process defined in the following document Card Validation Process for AFT (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237) 

One of the features, is validate that the name of the user and the name of the card matches with the in-house feature of "levenshtein" . See related task Add In house Name Matching algorithm to Card Validation with CRB integration (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4118) 

Finally, a  new feature was defined to allow to determine that the user is the owner of the card, which will be an extra "step" in the validation process in case the information returned by CRB is not sufficient to conclude that the card is valid.

The feature consist in performing a micro transaction of the OCT type, this means, to push funds to the card being validated, and then request to the user to enter the amount that was sent to the card, and review that the amount entered by the user, matches the amount sent.

The following details need to be taken into consideratin for this feature:
The transaction needs to be performed using the Push Funds solution from CRB, and it´s not going to be reversed (the funds will be "given away" to the user)
The transaction needs to have a RANDOM AMOUNT between 0.01 and 0.20 USD
The random amount needs to be obtained through a MORE ROBUST PROCESS than the algorith existing currently in the platform
If the users enter the correct amount the portion of the validatino flow will be considered succesfull and continue
If the user enter an incorrect amount he will be given a second and third opportunity to enter the correct amount
If the user fails to enter the amount 2 times, the outcome of this portion of the validation will be unsucessfull, and hence the card will be considered invalid
 
Due to the change in the API required fields the OCT Push API call needs to be executed including the following information for the required fields, specifically for the micro deposit scenario
When executing the MicroDeposit 
CorrespondingEntity field Data
needs to be fixed to =
FirsName = Fixed Value
LastName = Fixed Value
Account = Master Account / FBO Account # 
These values need to be adjusted to be configurations depending of the environment (QA/PROD) FBO Account
For QA (CRB Sandbox) the config should be as follows 
FirsName = Leap
LastName = Financial
Account = 0002345742163

Task Name: Add In house Name Matching algorithm to Card Validation with CRB integration
Task Content:
As part of the Card Validation Process to be implemented with the CRB integration, it is necessary to add a few extra steps to ensure that the card is Valid, when CRB does not provide enough information.

When the steps to validate the information of the card with the information provided by CRB are insufficient to determine if the card is Valid or not, the name matching feature of the Numi platform needs to be executed, considering the following outcome:

If the name matches with the current configuration of the name matching algorithm, the card is to be considered valid
when the name does not matches, an extra step needs to be executed to still intent to validate the card: A Micro Deposit (see rleated task: Perform OCT Push Micro Transaction as part of New Card Validation Process (https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4132) 

To see the full flow of the card validation solution see the following document = Card Validation Process for AFT (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237)

Task Name: [LP] Retry when sets destination payment method
Task Content:
Context
When the sender indicates that the recipient must fill in their information., there was a situation with the asyncronouse processing
When the recipient is using the landing port the issue was the following=
When the recipient filled their information in the landing port, as there is no interaction with the platform to get updates about the progress of the transaction flow, the landing port could assign the payment method to the transaction,  when the transaction was not in the correct status (OnHold/WaitingRecipientPaymentInfo), resuting in an error

Technical solution at Backend:
The API SetOperationPaymentMethodRequest will perform 3 retries automatically (during 30 seconds) to assign the payment method defined by the user. 
When these retries are "used", the API will send a response Status:Retry,  
 (this was done on DONE Nov 28).

Technical solution at RAAS:
As a result, the RaaS Middleware must also support the response with the new Status: Retry ( This was DONE Nov 28), to allow this solution also be handled by the Apps

Work to be Done
When the receiver user defines their payment method, either in the Landing Port or in the Ask App, if the transaction is not in the correct state to include the payment method information, the platform will attempt to retry with the payment method provided, during 30 seconds, and after that it will respond a retry state description, that the Landing Port or the Mobile Ask App need to handle

The handling was already implemented in Landing Port
When the Landing Port receives this status, it would request again. (this was done on DONE Nov 28).

The final part of this feature needs to be handled by mobile layer:
The Mobile Ask App needs to request te user to re-enter their payment method when the platform / RAAS middleware return the  Status:Retry, 
There is no limit for how many times the user can be requested to add the payment method informationm this means that  while the platform returns this Status Retry, the mobile layer needs to request the payment method information to the user.
Lists: [Technical Requirements - TA RaaS, 1.10 CINNAMON, Kanban R&D]
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text):
After the transaction is in the correct state to get the definition of the payment method information, and the landing port or the ask app layer, defines that information, correctly, the transaction can continue with the flow
For the recipient user, this will mean that the transaction will be completed sucessfully
If the transaction is not in the correct status, and the platform already executed retrieas automatically, the platform will expose a new state to the mobile layer, and the mobile layer needs to request the user to re-enter the destination payment information, as many times as necessary, until the payment method information can be added to the transaction information, in the platform.

Task Name: Cancel operations when Exchange Rate cannot be retrieved for several hours
Task Content:
When the Exchange Rate services are down, or not working, this causes the retrieval of the exchange rate to be executed in a series or retries
It was determined that if this retry mechanism is still not able to retrieve the exchange rate after 5 hours of attempts, the operation should be cancelled

Must configure the setting ExchangeRateRetriesTimesInMinutes: 60,60,60,60,60
It will retry every 60 minutes 5 times. 

In QA, you should define other values and change the setting EuroExchangeRateApiUrl to cause a communication error.

Note from TA 16.05.2025
This feature applies only when the exchange rate is needed for the actual cross border transaction. For the time being this operations are only with destination payment method = UBN, because the exchange rate is necessary to later on be used in the reports to finance to know the foreign currency that needs to be exchanged with the configured MSB

See related story for the exception for OCT that was identified later on release 1.11.7.9

Task Name: Implement EuroExchange as specific Exchange Rate Provider for Send to UBN flow
Task Content:
When the user wants to send money to their receiver using bank account as destination payment method, specifically for Mexico, using CLABE and executing the SPEI transaction through Arcus, the provider of the exchange rate will be different from the "regular" provider.

Currently the "regular" provider is Visa Exchange Rate service. This will continue to be the provider for the rest of types of destionation payment methods (e.g. send to a card)

Also, for the Pre-Quote, the information provided for the estimated cost of the transaction that is presented in the payment method selection screen, Visa Exchange Rate service will remain

EuroExchange will be the specific exchange rate provider for UBN in Mexico. This means that the transactions executed within this flow, the calculations of the amounts in both the QUOTE and the actual execution of the Transaction, will occur applying this exchange rate.

For Testing purposes it was informed that EuroExchange will have a fixed exchange rate or 0,62 under the regular Visa Exhgange:
EuroExchange = Visa Exchange - (Visa Exchange * 0,0062)

Task Name: Money Movement Report for Finance
Task Content:
When the different partners / providers require to move money to different Bank / Institution accounts, the Finance team needs to be able to define the amounts and destinations of the money movements.

For this reason, it is required to create a new report for Finance to process the Money Movements Correctly

The report should be provided DAILY, and should have all the relevant information for the operation money movement,, such as date, amount, and transaction type, plus the exchange rates involved in the operation life cicle, with the different amounts, to allow the finance team to identify the totals of money to move to the accounts involved in the daily settlement of the operations

The proposal from technical team is that this need will be covered with two reports :
1. An Operations Overall Money Movement Report
2. Am Operations Exchange Rate Delta Report

The Overal Money Movement report will serve to identify the different money movements involved in an operation, the identifier of the operation (operation id), the date and amount (with sign) of each movement, and the transaction type specific for the different movements. 
It was concluded that the report is very similar to the OCT Transactions report currently provided to MCB Bank, with certain specific information added, and certain other fields removed as they are not needed. 
Note: All the operations with all their involved transaction types will be displayed in this report

The Exchange Rate Delta Report will display the amounts and the exchange rates used in each case, in the different moments of the transaction life cycle, and provide the delta between the different moments the exchange rate was applied, if existing. 

Note: If no delta was identified between the original amount/exchange rate and the actually applied amount/exchange rate, the operation should not be displayed in this report.


Finally, there should be a section with "summarizes" the money flows, grouping the amounts by transaction type, to allow Finance to have a quick look of the money movements to be performed

See related document page with raw notes from this requirement and the videos from the requirement discovery sessions

See link with google sheet with an example and notes related to the information required (user tab named as Copy of exchange rate details example_ maintainUSD payment)

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NWsiCYGvP4M0QP7XKa_O9Q8h2o_uduvCpdZAW6cWNWY/edit?gid=2078570245#gid=2078570245

See attached .csv file for references
 
See the following video for fields explanation
https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/82beba53-77f2-4415-989a-12eec0e37d21/ExchangeRateDeltaReport%20explanation.mp4?view=open
Lists: [Technical Requirements - TA RaaS, Kanban R&D, 1.8.1 CYPRESS Bug Fixing]

Task Name: Enhacements to Name on Card Validation
Task Content:
Context
When a card is being added, a part of the validations performed is matching the name of the user´s profile with the given name of the card.
There were some validation errors that were actually not caused by the actual mismatch between the names being compared, but due to the handling of the comparison or the platform behaviour itself
It is necesary to add the corresponding mechanisms and enhacemtns to avoid this errors interfering with the actual name matching comparison.
Work to be Done
The details of the issues to be corrected is the following:
1) The handling of the matching comparison had a few issues found in the testing 
The lower case / upper case may interfere in the matching
The usage of special characters may interfere in the matching
2) When the user was recently created and the card is being validated right away, a conflict of platform events may cause that the user's profile is not yet updated to be able to use that name to match with the name on the card, causing an error in the validation that was not being handled
Latest Comment: @Vanesa V. Vilas  por favor validar si esto ya esta resuelto.
🤓 User Acceptance Criteria (text): The name on card should be validated properly and if any error arise, the error should only be related to the actual matching on the name, not exceptions or incorrect handling of the matching logic
